title: TexasHoldemEvent
description: Texas Hold'em-specific event payload, describing the `data` field of common/event.schema.json when the event occurred in a Texas Hold'em match. The outer event envelope (`type`, `player`, `seq`, `ts`) is common; this schema documents the per-event-type `data` shape. Discriminator is the outer `type` field. Mirrors games/texasholdem/texasholdem.go emission sites.

oneOf.description: new_hand — emitted at start of every hand (initial + subsequent). No `player` field on the outer envelope.

dealer.description: Player ID of the dealer this hand.

chips.description: Map of player_id -> pre-blind chip count.

oneOf.description: player_action — emitted whenever any player takes a betting action (including blinds). Outer envelope's `player` is the actor.

amount.description: Chips bet this action (delta). Present for blinds/call/raise/allin; omitted for fold/check.

total_bet.description: Player's total bet this round after this action.

oneOf.description: community_cards — emitted at flop/turn/river when cards are revealed. No `player`.
cards_dealt — (rare) emitted when hole cards are re-disclosed during reconnect scenarios. Outer envelope's `player` is the card owner.
hand_result — emitted at hand end. No `player`; pot distribution and winners listed in `data`.

winners.description: Player IDs who won (tie-split gives multiple entries).

pot.description: Total chips on the table this hand. Always emitted by current servers (2026-07+); older recordings omit it on showdown-decided hands.

reason.description: How the hand was decided: 'all_folded' or 'showdown'.

hand.description: 1-based number of the hand this result settles. Servers emit it from 2026-07; absent on older recorded events.

payouts.description: Map of player_id -> chips taken from the pot this hand (side-pot aware; only winners appear). Servers emit it from 2026-07; absent on older recorded events.

net_chips.description: Cash format only: map of player_id -> cumulative net chips across all completed hands INCLUDING this one — the figure the match is ranked on (same semantics as match_result.net_chips). Can be negative. Servers emit it from 2026-07; absent on older recorded events.

hands.description: Map of player_id -> {cards?: string[], hand?: string, folded?: bool}. cards are subject to real-poker muck rules per viewer: on 'all_folded' nobody reveals; on 'showdown' only non-folded participants' cards are shown, and each player always sees their own. 'hand' is the ranking name for showdown participants.

oneOf.description: match_result — emitted at match end (after final hand). No `player`.

winner.description: Match winner's player ID — cash format ranks by cumulative net (see net_chips), tournament by final chips. Empty string on a tie (see is_draw / winners).

winners.description: All player IDs tied for the best score (length > 1 means a draw). Always emitted by current servers.

is_draw.description: True when more than one player ties for the lead. Always emitted by current servers.

hand.description: Number of hands actually played. Always emitted by current servers.

reason.description: 'max_hands_reached' or 'opponent_eliminated' (tournament only; cash never eliminates). Always emitted by current servers.

chips.description: Map of player_id -> final chip stack. Always emitted by current servers. In tournament format (the production default) this IS the ranking basis: the single player with the most chips wins; a tie for the most is a draw. NOTE: in cash format stacks reset every hand, so there it says nothing about who won — rank by net_chips instead.

net_chips.description: Cash format only: map of player_id -> cumulative net chips across all hands — the figure the match is ranked on. Can be negative.

format.description: Present as 'cash' on cash-format matches; absent on tournament matches.

final_chips.description: Legacy field from pre-2026-06 servers; current servers emit 'chips' instead. Kept for old recordings.

oneOf.description: player_disconnected — emitted when a player is removed mid-match due to timeout/disconnect. Outer envelope's `player` is the disconnected player.
